description: When turning the full screen on or off, the game freezes for a while, about five seconds, and then the jukebox goes silent, and I don't notice anything else.\n\n

comments.body_text: This comment contained an image attachment, please login to view the attachment.
comments.rendered_body: This comment contained an image attachment, please login to view the attachment.

comments.body_text:
Please upload a video recording of the issue with the debug screen (F3) enabled. You can record your screen using:

* On Windows, the built-in Snipping Tool app (shift+win+S)


* On macOS, the the built-in Screenshot app (shift+cmd+5)


* Third-party software such as OBS



Consider placing a large, blank window (eg. Notepad or TextEdit) behind Minecraft while recording your desktop to protect your personal information.

It is recommended that you trim and/or reduce the quality of your recording so it is under 10MB, as this will make the upload much quicker.

comments.body_text:
It's weird, after I tried to do this again today, everything was fine.

Is it a problem with my computer or a compatibility issue?

content.text: It's weird, after I tried to do this again today, everything was fine.
Is it a problem with my computer or a compatibility issue?

comments.body_text:
I’m pretty sure the game momentarily freezing when fullscreen is toggled via F11 won’t be fixed since it’s a relatively minor issue and would involve some low-level tweaking in order to fix. (It’s also very system dependent- I noticed it more on my older gaming rig, but with my new laptop the hang time is essentially zero.) 5 seconds is a bit long, but that may have been a fluke do your computer using lots of resources at that moment or something. Music stopping after a fullscreen toggle is a more serious issue, but given that it isn’t happening consistently for you (anymore) I’m not sure it can be chalked up to problem with Minecraft’s code at this point.

Due to this, and because I cannot reproduce either issue myself, I will request this ticket to be closed as Cannot Reproduce. If the issue occurs for you again, my advice is to free up resources on your system by closing other apps, restarting, etc. Hopefully some of the rendering changes Mojang is making for Vibrant Visuals will improve the game’s performance for you, potentially mitigating this issue or eliminating it altogether. If the issue does persist, consider seeking out technical support, as this kind of issue isn’t really within Mojang’s purview.
